title: How Climate Change Will Affect What You Eat
subtitle: 气候变化将如何改变你的餐桌

paragraphs.en: Earlier this year, scientists warned that one in six animal species could go extinct due to climate change. Could the same thing happen to our crops and other foodstuffs too? It's clear that farmers in many parts of the world are going to find things harder in the coming decades.
paragraphs.zh: 今年早些时候，科学家警告称，六分之一的动物物种可能因气候变化而灭绝。同样的事情也会发生在我们种植的作物和其他食品上吗？很明显，在未来几十年里，世界许多地区的农民将面临更严峻的挑战。

paragraphs.en: Last week, BBC Future explored one scientist's efforts to help crops cope with the increased probability of droughts. By using the genes from resurrection plants, Jill Farrant of the University of Cape Town is exploring whether she can design crops to survive for much longer periods without water. But if we can't find ways to protect other foods, will they survive climate change?
paragraphs.zh: 上周，BBC未来频道报道了一位科学家为帮助作物应对日益频繁的干旱所做的努力。开普敦大学的吉尔·法兰特正利用复活植物的基因，探索能否培育出能在无水条件下存活更长时间的作物。但如果我们找不到保护其他食物的方法，它们能否经受住气候变化的考验？

paragraphs.en: Fortunately, there is some good news on this front. Despite alarmist headlines about "foods that are going extinct," there is no evidence that major food types like beans, chocolate, wine, corn or wheat will cease to exist. But that doesn't mean it's all good news for future food. We will probably have to change where we grow certain crops, as some regions get too hot. The disadvantage, obviously, is that local farmers will suffer under this situation. And some people may struggle to get the same access to certain foods.
paragraphs.zh: 幸运的是，这方面也有一些好消息。尽管有关于「食物即将灭绝」的危言耸听的大标题，但没有证据表明豆类、巧克力、葡萄酒、玉米或小麦等主要食物种类会彻底消失。但这并不意味着未来食品领域都是好消息。由于某些地区变得过于炎热，我们可能不得不改变某些作物的种植区域。明显的弊端是，当地农民将因此蒙受损失。而且有些人可能难以像现在一样方便地获取某些食物。

paragraphs.en: "Even if overall food production may be unaffected, food security can still be impacted," says Margaret Walsh, a scientist at the U.S. Department of Agriculture's Climate Change Program Office. In other words, even if a certain food is still grown on some corner of the Earth, it doesn't mean that everyone will continue to have the same degree of access as today.
paragraphs.zh: 美国农业部气候变化项目办公室科学家玛格丽特·沃尔什表示：「即使总体粮食生产可能不受影响，粮食安全仍可能受到冲击。」换句话说，即使某种食物仍在地球某个角落生长，也并不意味着所有人都能像今天一样轻易获得。

paragraphs.en: Overall, the yields of many foods, from staples to life-enhancing extras such as coffee and chocolate, will likely be impacted by climate change too. How those decreases will be felt will depend on the degree of warming and the crop in question, but in general, "anything over about 30°C is very bad for crops," says Wolfram Schlenker, an associate professor of international and public affairs at Columbia University. For example, statistical studies that he and a colleague built of corn and soybean production in the U.S. show a steep decline after crossing the 30°C temperature threshold.
paragraphs.zh: 总体而言，从主食到咖啡、巧克力等提升生活品质的额外食品，许多食物的产量都可能受到气候变化的影响。减产的程度取决于变暖幅度和具体作物种类，但一般来说，「任何超过30°C的温度对作物都非常不利，」哥伦比亚大学国际与公共事务副教授沃尔夫拉姆·施伦克表示。例如，他和同事建立的美国玉米和大豆产量统计模型显示，超过30°C的温度阈值后产量会急剧下降。

paragraphs.en: In the U.S. — the world's largest producer of corn and soybeans — farms can move north to some degree, Schlenker says. But eventually, yields will likely suffer because the soil north of Iowa declines in quality — a legacy of glacial expansion. Other studies, including studies of wheat in India and corn in Africa, also found that there is a threshold above which yields sharply decline: crops can adapt and move, but only to a point.
paragraphs.zh: 施伦克说，在美国这个全球最大的玉米和大豆生产国，农场可以在一定程度上向北迁移。但最终产量仍可能受损，因为爱荷华州以北的土壤质量较差——这是冰川扩张遗留下的问题。其他研究，包括对印度小麦和非洲玉米的研究，也发现存在一个产量急剧下降的阈值：作物可以适应和迁移，但程度有限。

paragraphs.en: "What's common to all studies is the finding that extreme heat is damaging to crop growth, although exact cutoffs vary by crop," Schlenker says. "If predictions for the end of the century are true, though, I think a lot of agricultural areas in the U.S. will see significant hits." Under current conditions, about 4% of the world's croplands experience drought in any given year, but by the end of the century those conditions are forecasted to jump to about 18% per year.
paragraphs.zh: 施伦克说：「所有研究的共同发现是极端高温会损害作物生长，尽管具体临界值因作物而异。不过，如果对本世纪末的预测成真，我认为美国许多农业区将遭受重创。」在当前条件下，全球每年约有4%的耕地遭遇干旱，但到本世纪末，这一比例预计将跃升至每年约18%。

paragraphs.en: Some studies indicate that horticulture crops — generally, everything besides staples — may be impacted most severely, largely because they tend to be confined to a smaller geographic area. Researcher Andrew Jarvis and his colleagues found that 80% of coffee-growing zones in Central America and Brazil could become unsuitable by 2050, for example, while climate change will likely have "great impacts" on cocoa production in West Africa. "High quality chocolate will be less available in the future, and if you want it, you'll have to pay a lot more for it," Jarvis says.
paragraphs.zh: 一些研究表明，园艺作物——通常指主食以外的一切作物——可能受到最严重的影响，这主要是因为它们往往局限于较小的地理区域内。例如，研究员安德鲁·贾维斯及其同事发现，到2050年，中美洲和巴西80%的咖啡种植区可能不再适宜种植，而气候变化可能会对西非的可可生产产生「巨大影响」。贾维斯说：「未来高质量的巧克力将更难买到，如果你想品尝，就得多花不少钱。」

paragraphs.en: This means that, for those who can afford it, some foods will simply cost them more in the future. But for poorer people, those same price jumps will likely cause certain foods to go extinct from their diets. "The more you reduce, the shorter the supply, and the higher the price will jump," Schlenker says.
paragraphs.zh: 这意味着，对那些负担得起的人来说，未来某些食品只会更贵。但对较贫困的人群而言，同样的价格飙升很可能会导致某些食物从他们的餐桌上消失。施伦克说：「减得越多，供应越少，价格就会跳得越高。」

paragraphs.en: Another potential climate change-induced problem is our dependence on commodity crops — wheat, soybeans, corn and rice — which currently provide humanity with 75% of its calories, either directly or indirectly through the animals we raise on those crops. Jarvis and his colleagues also found that, over the past five decades, the world has seen an increasing standardisation of diets; the foods we eat globally today are 36% more similar than they were in 1961.
paragraphs.zh: 气候变化可能引发的另一个问题是我们对商品作物的依赖——小麦、大豆、玉米和大米——目前它们直接或间接（通过喂养牲畜）为人类提供了75%的热量。贾维斯及其同事还发现，过去五十年来，全球饮食的标准化程度不断提高；今天我们全球食用的食物种类比1961年时相似度高出36%。

paragraphs.en: While this can be good news for the world's poorest people who now consume more calories, protein and fat than in the past, homogeneity and overdependence on a handful of staples leave us vulnerable to threats such as drought, disease and pests — all of which are predicted to worsen in many parts of the world as a result of climate change.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然这对世界上最贫困的人群来说可能是好消息——他们现在摄入的热量、蛋白质和脂肪都比过去多——但饮食的同质化和对少数主食的过度依赖使我们易受干旱、疾病和虫害等威胁的影响，而预计气候变化将导致这些威胁在全球许多地区加剧。

paragraphs.en: There are ways we could soften the coming blow to the global food supply, however. Like Farrant's work with resurrection crops, a number of companies, organisations and researchers are aiming to create drought- and temperature-resistant crops through genetic engineering and conventional breeding. For now, the jury is still out as to how successful those endeavours will be. "The people at Monsanto who I've talked to are much more optimistic that they'll be able to engineer heat-tolerant crops," Schlenker says. "On the other hand, scientists at the USDA who I've spoken with are much more cautious."
paragraphs.zh: 不过，我们有一些方法可以减轻全球粮食供应即将遭受的打击。与法兰特在复活作物方面的研究类似，许多公司、组织和研究人员正致力于通过基因工程和传统育种培育耐旱耐高温的作物。目前，这些努力能有多成功尚无定论。施伦克说：「我接触过的孟山都公司人员对于能培育出耐热作物要乐观得多。而另一方面，我交谈过的美国农业部科学家则谨慎得多。」

paragraphs.en: Until genetic engineering comes to fruition, other strategies might also help in some places, including applying more fertiliser, implementing better irrigation, using machinery that gets crops out of the field faster or installing storage facilities to delay spoilage. "Many places could benefit a great deal just by using technologies that already exist," Walsh says. "General farm management can go a long way toward easing changes."
paragraphs.zh: 在基因工程取得成果之前，其他策略在某些地方也可能有所帮助，包括施用更多肥料、改善灌溉系统、使用能更快收割作物的机械，或建设储存设施以延缓腐坏。沃尔什说：「许多地方仅通过使用现有技术就能受益匪浅。良好的农场管理对于缓解变化大有裨益。」

paragraphs.en: Finally, diversifying our diet away from heat-sensitive wheat, corn, rice and other crops could also help. "We've seen profound changes in the last decades in what we eat largely as a result of international trade, and I think that trend toward more diversification will continue," Jarvis says. "Depending on a greater number of plant species creates a more vigorous and less risky food system — and one that provides a broader range of nutritional requirements."
paragraphs.zh: 最后，让我们的饮食多样化，减少对热敏感的小麦、玉米、大米等作物的依赖，也会有所帮助。贾维斯说：「过去几十年来，受国际贸易影响，我们的饮食结构发生了深刻变化，我认为这种更加多样化的趋势将继续下去。依赖更多种类的植物物种，可以打造一个更有活力、风险更低的粮食系统——而且能提供更广泛的营养需求。」
